highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.216 Released
highlights.summary: This release fixes a significant performance bug where long sessions would slow down quadratically as conversations grew, causing multi-second stalls — great news if you use Claude Code for extended coding sessions. It also adds a new `sandbox.filesystem.disabled` setting that lets you skip filesystem isolation while still controlling network egress, giving advanced users more flexibility. Update to get noticeably faster session resumes and fewer frustrating pauses.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.215: /verify and /code-review Now Manual Only
highlights.summary: Claude Code no longer automatically runs the `/verify` and `/code-review` skills on its own — you now need to explicitly invoke them by typing `/verify` or `/code-review` when you want a review. This change gives you more control over when these potentially slow or noisy checks run, keeping your workflow uninterrupted. If you relied on automatic reviews, update your habits to call these commands manually.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.212: /fork Now Creates Background Sessions, /subtask Replaces Old Behavior
highlights.summary: The `/fork` command has been redesigned — it now copies your conversation into a new background session visible in `claude agents`, so you can branch work without interrupting your current session. What `/fork` used to do (launch an in-session subagent) is now handled by the new `/subtask` command. If you use multi-agent workflows, update your muscle memory and scripts to use the correct command for each use case.

highlights.title: Claude Code: Anatomy of a Misfeature
highlights.summary: This opinion piece critically examines a specific Claude Code feature that the author argues causes more harm than good, walking through how it was likely designed and why it misfires in practice. It's a thoughtful read for developers who want to understand the tool's rough edges and avoid common pitfalls. Reading critical analyses like this helps you use Claude Code more deliberately rather than blindly trusting its defaults.

highlights.title: Setting Up Your Spare Mac for Claude Code to Control
highlights.summary: This step-by-step guide walks you through configuring a spare Mac as a machine Claude Code can autonomously control, enabling hands-off agentic workflows. It's a practical setup guide for developers who want to let Claude handle longer-running tasks without tying up their primary machine. Even if you don't have a spare Mac, the guide's principles for safe agentic setup are valuable for any remote or sandboxed Claude Code configuration.

highlights.title: Cognikernel: Local Persistent Memory for AI Coding Assistants
highlights.summary: Cognikernel is an open-source tool that gives Claude Code (and other AI coding assistants) persistent local memory across sessions, so architectural decisions and context from past sessions aren't lost each time you start fresh. It addresses a common pain point where developers have to manually maintain `CLAUDE.md` files or markdown notes to preserve context. If you find yourself constantly re-explaining your project setup to Claude, this tool is worth trying.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial promises a fast, comprehensive overview of Claude Code's key features, making it ideal for developers who want to get productive quickly without sitting through a multi-hour course. It's a great starting point if you're new to Claude Code or want a refresher on features you might have missed. Watch it in one sitting to build a solid mental model before diving into longer, more detailed tutorials.

tips_of_the_day: Use `/code-review` explicitly at natural checkpoints in your work — such as after completing a feature or before a commit — rather than waiting for Claude to run it automatically, since v2.1.215 removed automatic invocation.
If your Claude Code sessions feel sluggish or slow to resume after many back-and-forth turns, upgrading to v2.1.216 should noticeably improve speed thanks to the quadratic message normalization fix.

Keep a well-maintained `CLAUDE.md` file at your project root with key architectural decisions, coding conventions, and context — it's the simplest way to give Claude persistent memory across sessions without any extra tooling.
